The grid storage industry set a wild goal for 2025 - and then crushed it

In 2017, U.S. grid storage developers promised they could deliver 35 gigawatts by 2025.
By Julian Spector

4 December 2025

In 2017, the early leaders in energy storage made an audacious bet: 35 gigawatts of the new grid technology would be installed in the United States by 2025.

The U.S. crossed the threshold of 35 gigawatts of battery installations this July and then passed 40 gigawatts in the third quarter.

Getting from basically no grid batteries to 35 gigawatts

Storage has become the dominant form of new power addition, Kathpal said.

The storage market ran into plenty of false starts, but real installations significantly outpaced the expected numbers in the run-up to 2025.

  • Developers built projects faster than early forecasts expected.
